entries.message: Added Release Notes section to admin reports
Fixed folder template selection when creating documents from a template
Support link in sidebar always shows as locked in production
Fixed: consultants blocked from saving incidents due to missing permissions check
Standard consultants and clients can now edit their own profile details
Fixed typo in phone number example on settings page
Fixed: profile changes on settings page not reflected until page refresh
Fixed: admins blocked from saving their profile due to spurious sources validation error
Updated empty folder message in template library
Removed Setup Wizard from template library
Feedback comments dialog now shows the original message at the top
Improved session expiry error message shown on file upload failure
Redesigned Template Library header — icon badge and action buttons always visible at top
Fixed: company name missing from sidebar immediately after login
Fixed: pro consultants' assigned sources not showing in company creation form
Admins can now permanently delete cases and incidents, with confirmation dialog and full audit trail
Consultants can now build and download PDF document bundles from a case's checklist items
Guardian Group logo on the login page now links to guardiangroup.co.uk
Pro consultants can now manage module access at both company and site level
Fixed: login could hang due to slow background data requests
Fixed: session timeout stays active while any tab is in use
Fixed: patch number no longer auto-increments on server restart
Fixed: company dropdown when adding a user now shows all companies, not just the first page
Fixed: all company dropdown filters now show the full company list
Fixed: company list no longer capped at 100 results
Improved: sites list now loads significantly faster
Improved: users and sites lists no longer re-fetch on first visit
Fixed: pro consultants assigned to sites now appear in the company Users list
Fixed: toolkit folder dropdown in Add Templates now always loads fresh data
Add Templates dialog now includes a Create New folder option inline
Client users are blocked from logging in when their company is On Hold or Inactive
Feedback & comments now preserve line breaks and paragraphs exactly as typed
Users can now edit their own feedback (when it has no upvotes or comments) and edit their own comments
Sites dropdown locks until a Company is selected
When a company or site address is updated, you are now prompted to copy the new address to any matching sites or the parent company
Users Report now distinguishes between Consultants and Pro Consultants — each shown with a distinct badge, colour, and separate summary count
Fixed: address updates now reflect immediately on the company's sites list
Fixed: site address on the company Sites tab now shows the full address including Address Line 2 and County
Added ACAS Conciliation as a case type in Employment Law — available in the new case form and case type filter
Admins can grant Case Advocate permission to consultants — only Case Advocates can view and manage Employment Law cases
Case Advocates only see cases matching their assigned sources
Admins can grant or revoke Template Library and Training Library access per consultant
Template and Training Library permission toggles appear in both the Permissions dialog and Create Consultant form
Fixed: non-active users are greyed out and unselectable in the Case Access dialog
Cases now have a mandatory Case Number field shown in the case list
Case Number can be edited from the case detail view; duplicates are blocked
Improved: Testing page redesigned with side-by-side panel layout for easier use
Testing: task lists can now be archived; archived view accessible from left panel
RIDDOR section moved from Report Incident form to Follow Up Investigation
Follow Up Investigation adds RIDDOR reference and notes fields
Incident detail view and PDF now display RIDDOR reference and notes
User list: new Permissions column shows colour-coded badges per consultant
User list: admin rows show an All badge in the Permissions column
User list: Case Advocate badge removed from Role cell; shown only in Permissions column
Settings: Your Permissions card moved to top with read-only permission toggles
Companies can now be linked to a Group Owner company
Group Owner companies show a Linked Companies panel with all member companies
Company list shows Group Owner and Member Of badges
Group Owner moved into Linked Companies panel — single unified section
Standalone companies now show clear options to join a group or become a Group Owner
Company list: filter by group shows the Group Owner and all its members
Patch version auto-increments on every production deployment
Patch headings now show the date of the latest entry in that patch
Template source filtering: restrict template visibility by source
Older invite links now show a clear "replaced by newer email" message
Sidebar permission-gated items now appear instantly on login (no refresh)
Dashboard now shows skeleton placeholders during initial load instead of flashing an empty state.
Dashboard now renders instantly with zeros that count up to real values
Site Documents page: filter by Group Owner with new Group + Company summary cards
Fixed: shared documents to a Group could not be uploaded
Documents button on Group/Company cards opens a list filtered to that scope
Site Documents filter bar now has an X to clear active filters
Client sign-off emails now go only to the consultant driving the approval
Companies, Sites and Users pages now share a consistent header and footer layout
Companies, Sites and Users pages now show a record count in the footer
Fixed: email document links now open the document after logging in
Activation page now shows email and username in a clear table
Activation/reset screens now use the branded login layout
Activation success screen no longer disappears when tab is refocused
Group/company documents now show a Shared list with add/remove (auto-updates compliance)
Fixed: shared-companies list crashed on group documents
Group-shared documents now appear and count for each shared company and its sites
Linked group documents now show on company document pages with a clear "Linked from Group" badge and read-only actions
Documents button on Group/Company tiles now opens a properly scoped Documents view with the Group or Company name in the header
Folders view now also respects Group/Company scope when opened from a tile, listing the scoped documents and linked items
Folder structure now also exists at Group and Company level, with auto-provisioned folders from templates and a folder selector available when uploading or creating from template in those scopes
Folders view at Group/Company scope now auto-creates the folder structure from templates and lists documents grouped by folder
Fixed folder creation so Group and Company folders are saved with the correct scope and stop showing as empty
Group Owner selection on the Site Documents pages is now remembered when navigating between modules

Shared Group/Company documents now appear inside the matching site folder with a dashed border and Shared tag, instead of in a separate Shared Documents area. Shared documents are read-only at site level and can only be edited at the parent (Group or Company) where they were created
Stopped production from auto-bumping its own changelog patch on startup; patch is now owned by dev and advances after each successful publish.
Added auto-detect on the dev Changelog page: it polls the live site once a minute and bumps dev's patch as soon as a new publish ships.
New public endpoint /api/changelog/published-patch returns the active version's current patch (used by dev's auto-detect).
Simplified Changelog header — removed the 'Live: vX.Y.ZZ' badge; each card now just shows the current patch number.
Added a User Logins admin report (Admin Reports → User Logins) showing successful sign-ins for any chosen date, defaulting to today.

Template Library: anyone with permission to see the page now has the same management access as Admins (Add Template, Add Folder, edit, archive/delete, drag-to-reorder, etc.). Previously these actions were Admin-only.

Client sign-off emails: stopped firing the 'No Consultant Assigned' admin email when a Pro consultant has already been notified. The system now (1) emails the consultant who uploaded the document, (2) falls back to any Pro consultant assigned to the site, and (3) only escalates to admins as a last resort when no consultant could be reached.
User Logins report: fixed a bug that caused the report to always show zero logins. The endpoint was checking the wrong user object and returning 403 to admins/consultants.
User Logins report: added date-range presets — Today (default), Last 3 days, Last 7 days, Last 30 days, plus a Custom range with From/To date pickers.
Admin Reports: added an Active Users Now tile that lists everyone with a current signed-in session, with a manual Refresh button and auto-refresh every 30 seconds while the dialog is open.
Admin Reports: moved Changelog / Release Notes onto its own dedicated page (/admin-reports/changelog) and added a tile to open it from the Admin Reports landing page.

Admin Reports & Changelog: locked down to administrators only. Removed consultants from the page-level access checks, the Active Users / User Logins / Changelog API endpoints, and the consultant sidebar link. Updated the page subtitle and confidentiality banner to say 'administrators only'.

Module Sites: replaced 'Overdue' stat on Group and Company tiles with 'Missing'. Group missing aggregates required template slots with no document across every site in every company belonging to the selected group. Company missing aggregates required template slots with no document across every site belonging to that company. Mirrors the All Sites tile's orange 'Missing' styling and badge logic.

Module Sites: corrected the 'Missing' count on Group and Company tiles. It now reports required templates with no document uploaded at THAT scope only — Group Missing = required templates from the group-owner company that lack a group-scoped doc, Company Missing = required templates that lack a company-scoped doc — instead of aggregating site-level missing across the group/company. Added a new GET /api/required-template-ids-by-company endpoint (filterable by module) that returns required template IDs grouped by accessible company; the tiles consume it to compute the count and the compliance % denominator.

Module Sites: made the Group and Company tile stat counts internally consistent. Compliant / Review / Missing are now all driven by the same template-slot iteration: for each required template at that scope, look up the doc uploaded at the matching strict scope (group: scope='group' && entityId=selectedGroup; company: scope='company' && entityId=company.id) and bucket it. Group-shared / inherited docs no longer leak into Compliant or Review on the Company tile, which fixes cases like Recreation Limited showing 2 Compliant + 1 Missing at 100% (numerator and denominator now agree). Overdue docs are not bucketed (they have a doc, so not Missing per the agreed definition).

Module Sites: 'Missing' on the Company tile now reflects only the company's OWN direct requirements. The /api/required-template-ids-by-company endpoint now filters companyRequiredTemplates to rows with inheritedFromCompanyId=null — so requirements that were cascaded down from a parent group are excluded from the per-company count. The Group tile is unaffected because it queries the group-owner companyId whose rows are direct (null) by construction. Site-level requirements are not (and were not) part of these counts; the All Sites tile is the place for those.

Fix Company tile Missing count to mirror Documents page (per-site canonical logic). For each company, count unique missing template IDs returned by /api/missing-required-templates (which already accounts for site-template overrides and shared/site-scope docs that fulfil a slot), instead of comparing only own company-scope docs to direct required-template rows. Compliant/Review now count visible docs (own + group-shared) by status. Reverts the previous overly aggressive inheritedFromCompanyId filter at the call site.

Align Group tile load behavior with Company tile so all counts populate together. Compliant/Review now count visible group-scope documents by status (instant, driven by the documents query). Missing now derives from the existing /api/missing-required-templates response (filtered by companyId === selectedGroup, deduped by templateId), so it loads in the same network round-trip as the Company tile's Missing instead of waiting for a separate /api/required-template-ids-by-company query that resolved a beat later. Removed the now-unused requiredTemplateIdsByCompany query from module-sites.tsx.

Fix: required documents added at a member Company were incorrectly bubbling up to the Group's Documents page (showing as Missing slots in the Group's folders/table). Requirements should only cascade DOWN (Group → member Companies → Sites), never up. The Group documents view (module-documents.tsx) was previously including missing slots from both the group entity and any member companies (filter checked m.companyId === urlEntityId || memberIds.includes(m.companyId)). Tightened the filter so the group scope only shows missing slots whose companyId matches the group owner company itself — i.e. requirements added at the Group level. Removed the now-unused groupMemberCompanyIds query (/api/companies?groupOwnerId=...) since nothing else on the page consumes it.

Fix: Site tile on Module Sites was over-counting Compliant when group/company-shared docs whose template wasn't in the site's required set were visible to the site (e.g. a group-scope compliant doc whose template the company never inherited because of a cascade gap, or a doc the site explicitly excludes via a site_template_override). Result: Compliant + Review + Overdue + Missing could exceed the site's effective required count. Now constrained: for each templateId in the site's effective required set we pick the best-status covering doc (compliant > review_required > overdue) and bucket once. Docs whose template isn't required at the site are ignored for slot accounting (they remain visible elsewhere). Added a new endpoint /api/effective-required-template-ids-by-site?module=X returning Record<siteId, string[]> after applying site-level overrides; uses getEffectiveCompanyRequiredTemplateIds + getSiteTemplateOverrides server-side, mirrors the active+private+module template filter from getMissingRequiredTemplateDetails. Module Sites Site tile now consumes that map and computes Compliant/Review/Overdue from per-template best-status; Missing remains driven by /api/missing-required-templates. With current data this changes Recreation Site 1 from 2C/0R/4M (total 6) to 1C/0R/4M (total 5), matching the site's effective required count of 5. Group and Company tiles unchanged.

Fix: Group→Member required-template cascade was non-binding. Per the rule "if a required document is added at group level, all companies and all sites below should require this document", this fixes both the existing data drift AND prevents future drift. Drift fixed via a new idempotent storage method backfillGroupRequiredTemplatesCascade() that, for each group owner, ensures every member company carries every group-required template with inherited_from_company_id set so future remove-cascades work; uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so existing rows (including member-owned rows that happen to match a group requirement) are preserved. Wired into server/index.ts startup right after seedToolkitRootFolders, non-fatal on failure, logs the inserted row count when > 0. On this restart it inserted exactly 1 row: "Employee Full Time Permanent Contract" for Recreation Limited (was missing despite Megabyte requiring it — historical gap from when member-level removal was permitted). Future drift prevented in storage.removeCompanyRequiredTemplate: now looks up the existing row first, and if the company has a groupOwnerId AND the row's inherited_from_company_id matches that group, throws an Error with code INHERITED_REMOVAL_FORBIDDEN and a clear message. The DELETE /api/companies/:companyId/required-templates/:templateId route catches that code and returns 400 with the message instead of 500/204. Group-owner-driven additions and removals continue to cascade as before. UI updated in client/src/pages/company-detail.tsx to match server contract: in the inline Required Documents list, the X button on inherited rows is now disabled with a tooltip explaining the requirement must be removed at the group level; in the Manage Requirements dialog, inherited templates show a checkbox that is checked-and-disabled with an Inherited badge and the same tooltip, so users can no longer untick them. The pre-existing AlertDialog confirmation flows for inherited removal become dead paths (kept in place for safety; they would only fire if the disabled control were bypassed, which isn't possible from the UI). Site-level overrides via site_template_overrides are unchanged: sites can still include/exclude templates including inherited ones (consistent with previous turn where Site 1 excluded Employee Part Time). Net effect end-to-end: Recreation Limited's Required Documents list now shows Employee Full Time (inherited badge); every Recreation site picks it up dynamically through getEffectiveCompanyRequiredTemplateIds; sites' missing-required and Site tile compliance counts now reflect it; and any future attempt to drop an inherited row at a member company is blocked at both the API and UI layers.

Soft-removed group-required cascade: when a parent group drops a required template, member companies and their sites now keep that template visible in their Required Documents lists as a struck-through 'No longer required by parent group' entry (with the Inherited badge), instead of silently disappearing — so users can see what was removed without losing context. The soft-removed rows do not count toward compliance or Missing tiles, and re-adding the template at the group level cleanly reactivates them everywhere. Group-owner own rows and member-managed (non-inherited) rows still hard-delete on removal as before.

Inherited required documents are now removable at the member company level. Clicking the X on an active inherited row soft-removes it (struck-through with the Inherited badge and 'No longer required by parent group' subtitle) for that company and its sites; clicking X again on an already-soft-removed row permanently deletes the entry from this member only. Re-ticking in Manage Requirements (or re-adding at the group level) reactivates it. Removed the previous member-level removal block (INHERITED_REMOVAL_FORBIDDEN) and the related confirmation dialogs since the action is now reversible.

Soft-removed inherited requirements on a company can now be re-enabled. The X button on a struck-through (soft-removed) row in a company's Required Documents list is replaced with a Restore button (counter-clockwise arrow) — clicking it reactivates the requirement (clears the soft-removed flag) and counts toward compliance again. Previously, clicking the X on an already-soft-removed row would permanently delete the entry, leaving no in-place way to bring it back; now the only way to permanently delete from the list is via the group level.

GDPR name redaction for incident reports: personal names (affected person, reporting person, declaration, submitted-by, witnesses, investigation-completed-by, RIDDOR responsible person, and structured investigation witness entries) are now anonymised to '[Redacted]' server-side for any user whose role is not 'client'. Clients who submitted the report see full names. Consultants and admins see redacted names across the incident list, incident detail, and investigation report HTML endpoints. The original stored HTML report (generated at submission time) retains full names as the source-of-record document.

Extended GDPR redaction on incident reports to also cover address fields: affectedPersonAddress and reportingPersonAddress are now redacted to '[Redacted]' for non-client users, alongside the already-redacted name fields.

Consultants assigned to a site now receive an automatic email notification when a client submits an incident report for that site. The email includes the incident reference, company and site name, incident type, severity (with colour-coded badge), and date — no personal names or addresses in accordance with GDPR. The notification fires in the background (fire-and-forget) so the client's form submission is never delayed.

After a client submits an incident report, a confirmation dialog is shown displaying the incident reference and the full names of all assigned consultants who were notified. The server now includes notifiedConsultants in the POST /api/incidents response (fast DB lookups, no delay to submission). If no consultants are assigned, the dialog still shows with a clear note.
Added portal_messages table to schema and storage layer with full CRUD (create, read, update, delete); implemented getPortalMessages with publishedOnly and role-filter options

Added GET /api/home-summary endpoint: returns urgentActions (overdue/review/pendingApprovals/openIncidents/pendingSignOffs), portfolio (assignedSites/companies/cases/sources for consultants+admins; site+primaryConsultant for clients), and portalMessages visible to user role
Added GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/portal-messages routes; admins see all messages, other roles see published+non-expired+role-targeted messages only
Created /home page (portal home) with greeting, urgent actions panel, my portfolio panel (role-aware), quick links, and portal messages grid; / and /home both map to the home page
Moved existing dashboard to /dashboard route; updated sidebar Overview section with Home link (/) and Dashboard link (/dashboard); added Portal Messages to admin navigation
Created /admin/portal-messages page for admins to create, edit, publish/unpublish, pin/unpin, and delete portal messages broadcast to portal users
Fix home page: / redirect to /home, date in greeting, client doc links, admin access requests count, portal messages table layout, audience selector, remove as-any casts

Fix home page review issues: type badge + correct colors on portal message cards, publishedAt defaults to now in admin form, typed SQL in home-summary route (remove as any), client incidents filter by reported_by, parameterized role SQL in storage
Add /api/home-summary/items endpoint for urgent action modal drill-down (overdue docs, incidents, access requests etc.)
Remove Quick Links card from home page (redundant with sidebar navigation)
Urgent Actions: clicking a metric now opens a modal listing specific items with View links, replacing direct navigation
Redesign My Portfolio panel: stats row (Companies/Sites/Cases), client list with site counts, improved visual hierarchy
Client Account panel redesign: avatar icons, divider layout, document/incident quick-action buttons
Add Zod schema validation to PATCH /api/portal-messages/:id to constrain mutable fields
Add SQL migration file 0005_add_portal_messages.sql for reproducible portal_messages table creation
Fix portal message create/update: coerce ISO string timestamps to Date before schema validation to match Drizzle types
Fix PATCH /api/portal-messages/:id: use z.preprocess for date fields so omitted fields remain undefined (not coerced to null)
Add CTA fields to portal_messages (cta_type, cta_url, cta_label): DB columns, schema, PATCH validation, admin UI dropdown+inputs, modal CTA button with gradient styling
Fix portal messages: add published_at <= NOW() visibility gate so future-dated messages are not shown until their scheduled publish time
Move cases out of My Portfolio: remove Cases stat tile from portfolio card; add Open Cases urgent action row (admin/consultant) with teal styling and drill-down modal linking to /employment-law/cases
Equalise Urgent Actions and My Portfolio card heights: grid items-start, portfolio shows 4 clients by default with chevron expand/collapse toggle; portal messages display higher on page

Add client-controlled toggle on incident detail page granting consultants/admins full (unredacted) access to personal info on incident reports. Includes dedicated PATCH /api/incidents/:id/consultant-access endpoint (client-only), per-incident redaction bypass in list/detail/investigation-report endpoints, consultant_full_access boolean column, sidebar toggle card for clients, and emerald access-granted banner for consultants/admins.

Site-level Required Documents: inherited docs now show a strikethrough "Not required at this site" state (matching company-level behavior) when marked with X, with a RotateCcw restore button to reinstate them; Manage Requirements dialog updated to reflect excluded-but-inherited state correctly.
Case bundle PDF: page numbers now start at 1 (was 0-based) and render in black (was grey).

Case bundle PDF: use an explicit PostScript counter for page numbers (pop built-in arg, init to 0, increment per page) so numbering reliably starts at 1 regardless of Ghostscript BeginPage convention.

Case bundle PDF page numbers: fix dictionary scoping bug where def inside bound BeginPage procedure created a local shadow instead of updating the outer counter. Now uses userdict /pagenum put/get explicitly, starting at 1, showing before incrementing.
Case bundle: cleared cached bundle URLs in DB so all bundles regenerate fresh on next download, picking up the corrected page numbering (black, starting at 1).

Case bundle PDF: support .msg (Outlook email) file conversion via LibreOffice. Added application/vnd.ms-outlook MIME mapping; convertFileToPdf now accepts optional fileName to fall back on file extension when browser sends application/octet-stream for .msg uploads.
Case bundle: cleared bundle cache a second time (it re-built before filename-fallback code was live); added conversion log to confirm .msg extension resolution fires correctly on next download.

Case bundle: cleared bundle cache a third time after confirming filename-fallback code is live; direct test confirmed application/octet-stream + .msg filename correctly resolves to msg extension for LibreOffice.

Case bundle: fix .msg conversion. Real Outlook .msg files are OLE2 binary format which LibreOffice cannot open. Added server/msg_to_html.py (uses extract-msg Python library) to convert .msg -> HTML first, then LibreOffice converts HTML -> PDF. Installed extract-msg Python package.

Case bundle .msg fix: forced server restart to load new msg_to_html.py integration; cleared bundle cache. End-to-end test confirmed: extract-msg Python → HTML → LibreOffice → 64KB PDF works on real OLE .msg file.

.msg bundle conversion: switched from LibreOffice HTML export (Letter size) to weasyprint PDF generation with @page { size: A4; margin: 2cm } CSS — produces correct 595x842pt A4 output. Installed weasyprint Python package.

.msg conversion: replaced Outlook HTML body rendering (which caused multi-page/non-A4 output) with clean plain-text extraction. Headers + body now flow together on A4 pages, matching expected format. Emoji/unmappable characters stripped for clean output.
